class.name: FmodSound
class.brief_description: A reference-counted wrapper around FMOD::Sound representing a loaded audio resource.
class.description:
FmodSound is a RefCounted wrapper around FMOD::Sound, representing a loaded sound resource. It provides access to sound metadata, format information, raw audio data, music-specific controls for tracker formats (MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI), and metadata tags.
Use [method load_from_file] to load a sound from disk, or create one through [FmodSystem] methods. Once loaded, you can query information about the sound, read raw audio data, or use it with [FmodChannel] for playback.

method.name: get_format
method.description: Returns a dictionary containing the sound's format information with the following keys: - [code]type[/code]: The audio format type ([enum FmodSoundType]). - [code]format[/code]: The sample format ([enum FmodSoundFormat]). - [code]channels[/code]: Number of channels (int). - [code]bits[/code]: Bits per sample (int).

method.name: get_length
method.description: Returns the length of the sound in the specified time units. When using milliseconds ([code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_MS[/code]), the result is returned in seconds. For other units like bytes or PCM samples, the raw value is returned. [b]Note:[/b] See [enum FmodSystem.FmodTimeUnit] for available time unit options.

method.name: get_music_channel_volume
method.description: Returns the volume of a specific channel in a MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI file, in decibels (dB). [b]Note:[/b] This only works for module music formats. Use [method get_music_num_channels] to get the number of available channels.

method.name: get_music_num_channels
method.description: Returns the number of channels in a MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI file. Each channel corresponds to a track/instrument in the module file. [b]Note:[/b] This only works for module music formats (tracker formats).

method.name: get_music_speed
method.description: Returns the relative playback speed of MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI music. A value of 1.0 represents normal speed, values less than 1.0 are slower, and values greater than 1.0 are faster. [b]Note:[/b] This only works for module music formats.

method.name: get_name
method.description: Returns the name of the sound. This is typically the filename or a name embedded in the audio file.

method.name: get_num_sub_sounds
method.description: Returns the number of sub-sounds in a multi-sound file. This is useful for formats like FSB (FMOD Sound Bank) or DLS (Downloadable Sounds) that contain multiple audio samples. [b]Note:[/b] Regular audio files like MP3 or WAV typically return 0 for this value.

method.name: get_num_tags
method.description:
Returns a dictionary with metadata tag counts: - [code]num_tags[/code]: Total number of tags in the sound. - [code]num_tags_updated[/code]: Number of tags that have been updated (for streaming sources).
[b]Note:[/b] Use [method get_tag] to retrieve individual tag information.

method.name: get_open_state
method.description: Returns the current open state of the sound. This is useful for sounds opened with non-blocking flags or for monitoring streaming buffer status. Returns a dictionary with: - [code]open_state[/code]: The current open state ([enum FmodOpenState]). - [code]percent_buffered[/code]: Percentage of the buffer filled (0-100) for streaming sounds. - [code]starving[/code]: [code]true[/code] if the stream is starving for data (buffer underrun). - [code]disk_busy[/code]: [code]true[/code] if the disk is currently busy reading.

method.name: get_sync_point
method.description:
Returns a sync point handle at the specified index. Sync points are embedded markers in audio files (e.g., loop points in WAV or cue points).
[b]Note:[/b] Use [method get_sync_point_info] to get information about the returned sync point handle.

method.name: get_sync_point_info
method.description: Returns information about a sync point. The [code]point[/code] parameter should be a handle obtained from [method get_sync_point]. Returns a dictionary with: - [code]name[/code]: Name of the sync point. - [code]offset[/code]: Position of the sync point in the specified time units. - [code]time_unit[/code]: The time unit used for the offset. - [code]pointer[/code]: The sync point handle.

method.name: get_tag
method.description:
Retrieves a metadata tag from the sound. Tags can be retrieved by index or by name. If [code]name[/code] is non-empty, it will search for a tag with that name; otherwise, it retrieves the tag at the specified index.
Returns a dictionary with: - [code]type[/code]: Tag type ([enum FmodTagType]). - [code]datatype[/code]: Data type of the tag value ([enum FmodTagDataType]). - [code]name[/code]: Name of the tag. - [code]data[/code]: The tag value (type varies based on [code]datatype[/code]). - [code]data_len[/code]: Length of the data in bytes. - [code]updated[/code]: [code]true[/code] if the tag was updated (for streaming sources).
[b]Note:[/b] Common tags include "TITLE", "ARTIST", "ALBUM" for music files.

method.name: load_from_file
method.description:
Static method to load a sound from a file path. The path should be a valid file system path or a Godot resource path (res://).
[b]Example:[/b] [codeblock] var sound = FmodSound.load_from_file("res://music.mp3") [/codeblock]

method.name: lock
method.description:
Locks a region of the sound's sample data for direct access. This allows reading or modifying the raw PCM data. Returns an [FmodSoundLock] object that provides access to the locked memory region.
[b]Note:[/b] Always ensure the [FmodSoundLock] is freed (goes out of scope) to unlock the data.
[b]Example:[/b] [codeblock] var lock = sound.lock(0, sound.get_length(FmodSystem.FMOD_TIME_UNIT_PCM)) # Access data through lock object [/codeblock]

method.name: read_data
method.description:
Reads raw audio data from the sound into a PackedByteArray using FMOD's internal codec. This is useful for extracting decoded PCM data or for custom processing.
[b]Note:[/b] Use [method seek_data] to position the read cursor before reading.

method.name: seek_data
method.description:
Seeks to a specific PCM position in the sound for data reading operations. The position is specified in PCM samples.
[b]Note:[/b] This affects the position used by [method read_data], not the playback position.

method.name: set_music_channel_volume
method.description:
Sets the volume of a specific channel in a MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI file, in decibels (dB). This allows individual control over each track/instrument in module music.
[b]Note:[/b] This only works for module music formats. Valid channel indices are 0 to [method get_music_num_channels] - 1.

method.name: set_music_speed
method.description:
Sets the relative playback speed for MOD/S3M/XM/IT/MIDI music. A value of 1.0 is normal speed, 0.5 is half speed, 2.0 is double speed, etc.
[b]Note:[/b] This only works for module music formats (tracker formats).

constants.constant: User-created sound with custom read callback.
Sound is ready to be played.
An error occurred while opening the sound.
Buffering data from a network stream.
Sound is in a "playing" state (for streams).
